Narrative_Meaning: Tens indicate a completion, whether the actual end of the story or the end of a character or phase.

Yarn.Yarn_Description: The Loner is the card of someone who while they may appear to succeed in some aspects of life, they are often failing in others. They may be rich, but lonely, or have many friends, but no true love. They may not feel they lack anything, and are content, but still are pitied by others. Then again the loner may seek solitude, moving far from home (to a new location), or driving others away for their own safety, as they know that what they undertake is not safe for others to assist. It is a card of collateral damage, Pyrrhic victories and great costs, but also huge rewards, if potential dangers are limited by careful planning.

Significator.Plot: The Loner Plot is unique and unconnected to other Plots and events. It is a one off, freak Plot, that may centre around an unusual individual (less often a small group).  The Character is usually socially awkward or even socio — or psychopathic in nature. Loner plots are light on Revelations, and almost always leave open questions behind them (such as what was that about? Who was that guy?).
Significator.Side: Sides of a plot marked by the Loner are not actually a side so much as a collection of individuals, they share no common goals, and may even be separate Sides effectively.
Significator.Character:
The Loner Significator indicates a Character who does not really enjoy the company of others.

Gain Yin when you are alone.

Gain Yang when you ignore others, or try to drive others away.

Gain Chi when you move away from others, or they move away from you.

They also gain +1 Success Level when acting on their own, or without backup.
Significator.Scene: The Loner Scene is about isolation. A Yarn-Teller may play it to isolate Characters from each other, to drive wedges between them, or to simply separate them for a time.

Yarn.Aspect_Description: The Hook targets the Character's Geometry particularly their Chi Gain offering them an easy source of Geometry Chi.
Yarn.Hook_Description: The Hook is a direct call to help from someone (usually at least a Cast Extra) known to the Character often an Ally or Friend. The person calling for help usually believes (typically with good reason) that only the Character will help.
Yarn.Fray_Description: A Complex Ordeal for a single Character (or small group), where as well as trying to get through enemy territory (and accomplish some goal beyond survival), the Character will also be fighting a spread out, but vastly numerically superior force. Individual enemies may be weaker or similar in power level to Lone Wolf Character. The fight may be limited to Medium Stakes in some cases (such as during training exercises, or in cultures where firearms and blades are unfashionable), but is usually High, or Extreme Stakes.
Yarn.Snag_Description: After the Fray, the Embodiment should realise the cost of the battle was too high. Maybe there are too many dead on their side, too many dead on the other — maybe seeing a Cast/Chorus Extra from an earlier scene, now lying dead. There is always collateral damage, so even if everyone is alive, their ancestral home is collapsing in flames behind them.

NPC.Description: The Miser is a lonely figure that loves their money more than any person in their life. They are usually quite rich, but do not like spending their money (which is often why they have so much).

Yarn.Sweeping_Text: The Pressed Embodiment achieves some amount of independence in comparison to before. This may mean they move out of a guardian's home (gain Location), get a new job (gain Wages Treasure Descendant), it may also represent the Resolution of a Dependence Hitch.
Yarn.History_Description: The Loner indicates that the current situation is rooted in past problems of isolation, but this isolation has now come to an end. A character who was isolated, imprisoned, or exiled by a corrupt lord now seeks revenge, or a location or knowledge that was isolated, and unknown, is discovered and made public, bringing this new problem.
Yarn.Situation_Description: The Loner indicates that the current situation has been caused by a character, location, vehicle or object being abandoned, deserted or discarded. Where previously they had companionship they are now alone, where they had life only ghosts remain, or where once they were treasured, now they are forgotten and in disrepair.
Yarn.Solution_Description: The Loner indicates that there is a route out of the current situation, but it is a path that can only be walked by a single person. Sometimes this means abandoning you companions to succeed, other times it means scattering so as to not draw attention together.

Revelations.About_Description: The information is about the background, or setting, not the current plot or situation. It might include pertinent details for the current plot, but is usually just about the problems faced by the majority (or even a minority) of people. These issues can range from obvious such as finding potable water, and edible food, to obscurities like the feather headdresses worn by the nobility must be properly oiled daily.
Revelations.Info_Description: Information about the issues a character is facing, this can be news that is commonly known ("His wife just died"), or something considered secret ("His doctor told me, in strictest confidence, of course, that it isn't actually a physical problem, nothing mechanically wrong, if you get my drift?")
Revelations.Vector_Description: The information is conveyed by voice, it may be an overheard conversation, a chatty taxi driver, or simply crop up as a topic during a morning exchange, and can, of course, even appear as nothing but rumour, gossip and hearsay. There is often a lot of nonsense that surrounds and may even occlude the revelation with red herrings and misinformation. Sometimes, for example, the voices in your head tell you something, the truth revealed then might be intuition, or something more, or perhaps you just overheard it somewhere.
Revelations.Alternate_Description: The information is about a Gain that a Character might make, or how to gain the Descendant or Location. You might need to know how to gain an Ally, or how to retrieve an artefact of ancient power.
Revelations.Detail_Description: Social details tell us about a person by the company they keep, are they a member of a church, a charity, a guild or other professional body, did they attend a certain school, join a secret society, a book, fan, or gentleman's club, or are they a member of an owners association?

Obstacle.Description: This way is blocked, by a Solitary Guardian, perhaps a Samurai, or maybe a sewer dragon.

Tarot.Crossed: Automatic Success +4 Additional Success Levels/ Draw Maximum Roll +4 Ordeal Cards
Tarot.Normal: Target fails with a Disaster (Score of -1 and 4 Failure Levels)/ -5 Cards.

Ordeal.Obstacles: 1 Obstacle in each Stage with 2 in all Court Stages.
Ordeal.Stage_Description: A very long, wide-open fields, spaces, straight paths, roads, or highways. Very long, hard, days of labour, research or travel.
Ordeal.Motional_Description: Get through the Stages before the time limit (limited in Rounds), on foot, or face serious consequences.
Ordeal.Tide_of_Battle_Text: The Flowing forces have learned the tactics of the Ebbing enemy. This advantage can allow the Flowing forces to discard half of all prepared Style Reserve cards from one Ebbing Style Reserve.
Ordeal.Fight_Description: Attacked by a Group at least as powerful as the PCs, but greater in number (usually by one or two).

Lea.Example: Cave Bear / Dinosaurs / Mystery Animals / Cryptids
Lea.Notes: These Paths are often lost, causing the Bulmäs that use them to appear cryptozoological.

Stress.Description: High Stress for hours, days, weeks or perhaps even years can profoundly alter a Character. Some people freak out if their schedule is disturbed even slightly, others need their sleep cycle disturbed before it becomes a problem. Others find themselves physically or emotionally disturbed. But this all amounts to the same thing.
Stress.Rules: A Character who has become disturbed (by the latest Stressor) cannot rest or settle while this latest stressor continues. If they can find a way to cease the disturbance they will relieve all current Stress (but not Strain or Shock) and cease being disturbed, but until that time they will add their current Stress and the Tension-Level as a Chi Difficulty to all Actions that are not based on ceasing the disturbance (or at least getting far away). Even Actions that act to cease the disturbance will add the Character’s current Stress in Chi as additional Difficulty.

Trauma.Description: Trauma can cause a Character to change, become insular and introverted, hateful and anti-social, or they can come to their senses and realise that some people they call friends are not really their friends at all. Whatever the reason, whether betrayal, hazing, secrecy, or mental illness, this Trauma urges a Character to think about who their friends and Allies are very carefully.
Trauma.Rules: The Character may remove friends and allies from their life (usually without violence, although there may be tears, accusations, and harsh words). Each friend or Ally removed will relieve Stress equal to the Character Type ("unfriending" a Chorus Extra would relieve 5 Stress).

Age.Description: An Age of Isolation is occuring. During this age a society reduces connections with other societies, this may be an active choice to limit association, or this can be an ostrasization, where they are forced out of contact by an external factor. This insularity within a society will always have a deleterious effect, resulting in stagnation, contraction and occasionally collapse.
Age.Rules: Connections to other societies are lost, restricted or limited, usually caused by external factors such as criminal enterprises, larger politics, and geo-politcal events such as wars.
